transcript.text: Thank you.
Thank you.
Hey, Bailey.
So sorry for being late.
No, no worries.
How's it going?
Thank you.
Good, good.
This is, it's like a weird time of year, and we usually don't have many meetings, but we have three meetings back-to-back this morning.
You look very cozy, like you're dressed for the season, with the trees and like the matching sweater.
Yeah, thank you.
I'm also, I'm usually in Seattle, but I'm out east today, in Baltimore, and it's like freezing, and it's snowy, and so it feels right.
Hey, Azzam, how's it going?
Pretty well, how about yourself?
Good.
I was just commenting on Steve's winter-appropriate outfit.
Yeah, no, it's very good.
very, it's very Seattle, think, is what you were saying.
It's spot on.
Like a Christmas tree.
Yeah, all that would be more Seattle, would be like a Patagonia vest, instead of the green.
I'm not in D.C.
I think Vermont has the same dress code.
It's like flannel in Patagonia.
Nice.
All right, I am going to jump in and share the agenda for
I know our agendas have been quite long for the last couple of weeks, but there's just been a lot to kind of circle up on.
So I think I mentioned to Azzam last week, but we have a bit of a quiet week from Christmas to New Year's.

So just to let you know, I'm going to plan to send an async doc this Friday that will just kind of let you know what's happening when, because I'm actually going to be out of office for two weeks over the holidays.
I'm going to be on a boat in the Galapagos.
very, yeah, it's a very exciting, but I want you to have a sense of kind of what's happening when and in the process and also who to contact you.
If you have questions and you may hear from Rachel, who is the managing editor, who is kind of going to be running the next round of content we're working on.
So if she pops up in Slack, that's who she is and you don't need to be surprised.
And so she may.
have questions or if you have feedback, she may handle that while I'm out just to make sure we're moving things along.
Let's see, maybe we start with some ops questions.
So we are working on our new pipeline.
Thank you for confirming the info on the logic flows, Azzam.
So our engineering team is going to use those to actually build them into our pipeline in a more automated way going forward.
So they may have questions, in which case I will connect you with them.
But hopefully I think we have everything we need there.
Same thing with the FAQ.
We've been adding that manually after the article, but we're going to build that in as its own step.

And then I think the last thing I had a question for is we have the ability to automate publishing or automate staging, which makes things a little more streamlined on our end, because it sort of takes the publishing ops person who puts articles up manually out of the process and means we can do it in a more automated way on our own.
So, if that's something you're open to, the one thing we need is an API key to connect Atlas, which is our kind of content generation pipeline to your blog.
So, that was the one thing that we would need for you to share with us, if that would be okay.
Yeah.
Awesome.
I think, like, the API key part you can speak to.
Happy to give you an API key.
If it makes things easier on your side, yeah, let's go for it.
I was going to ask Azam, how much are you still, do you find yourself still kind of proofreading or reading through everything before it goes live?
Yeah, I was going to say, like, how would that work with us?

Like, I would love to still be in the loop reviewing every article before they go live, especially as we take this new direction and, like, have a new content stream started to open up to a new persona.
I would expect, at least for the first month or so, want to review them all.
Get a sense of what's going out there, and make sure that it makes sense of the way we want to speak about it.
How might this work with a human in the loop still?

Yeah, it actually makes it easier, because it means basically our managing editor who's generating all of the content in Atlas, it just then becomes one other step in Atlas where she hits play, and then it stages to your CMS, and then she can just let you know that it's there and ready to be reviewed.

And what happens right now is she finishes all the work, she creates a ticket to ask our publishing ops person to manually do that, and then he has to kind of put it in his queue, and then let her know when it's been done.
So it just speeds up the whole process significantly, because he's manually publishing for a lot of clients.
So it just means that instead of having to go through that whole queue, we can just press a button, and then it stages in your CMS for review.
So from your perspective, the experience doesn't have to be any different, except that it can be much more instant.

Eventually as the goal, I feel like we want to get some images that you're happy with so that the whole thing becomes automated and you don't need to, like, add them from your side on that stage of the process.

So maybe when we're back from the holidays, we could take a look at that as well because I looked at the images that we were making for you and I feel like there's a lot of room for improvement there.
And I also, we have a new image generation pipeline, which I think is just generally making much better images and doing better work.
So I think we could revisit that as well.
Cool.

Yeah, and I think right now we don't, maybe we incorporate it in, like, the social, like, if you share it on social, we might incorporate the images, but, like, in the content itself, we, I don't think we use the images anywhere.
And that's not, that's not necessarily reflective of the images.
It's just, we just never updated the blog to write it.
But, yeah, we can definitely do better.
Well, I've also looked at the images and I, I don't know, I personally feel like there, there's room for improvement.
I think sometimes the the text.
I feel like it's sometimes that especially the text isn't doing like a great job capturing the main idea of the blog post.

And I think it's also a question on depending on how it looks in the blog, like whether you want the title on an image when like the title is right below it or whether you're better off with going something more visual.
So I think it's like, again, it's not super urgent.

I think they would be fine and functional, but if we're kind of doing this pivot, which is kind of like a rebrand of your content, it's another opportunity, I think, to take a look at what we're doing and see if it can be improved.
For what it's worth, think images are really nice shareables on social.
so if like either the title or some kind of hook was in the image, I think that'd be totally fine.
Yeah, one of ways I've seen this work is like there's actually two images.

One is the one that's actually displayed on the blog page itself, and the other is like the metadata image that includes the title so that when you share it elsewhere without actually opening the URL itself, it'll show the title on the image, if that makes sense.
I made that sound really complicated.
No, no, no, That's...
That totally makes sense.
I think that's pretty easy and pretty standard to have one image be like your thumbnail meta with the meta title on it and the other one be what you see on the blog page itself.
Have them being like related images, but not exactly the same.

Yeah, mean, I've seen them, like for us in the past, we've used the same image or just without text so that the one, you don't see it like twice in a row next to each other when you open the actual link.
Yeah, exactly.

And I think the current images we have, too, with the two lines of text, like even then, I think we'd probably want to have two different images because I think it would look pretty crowded on mobile as well.
So I'll add that to my list of things to take a look at.
And speaking of that, are you planning any kind of visual rebranding to go with kind of your pivot or like colors?
Not right now.
Okay, awesome.
Hopefully someday, but not yet.
Okay, so we updated the artifacts based on all your feedback.
Thank you, Azam, for going.
through those so carefully.
The one thing I wanted to circle back on was the sort of logic definition, since I know that was something that you had wanted to take offline and think about a little bit more.
So I just want to make sure if that's something that's changed, that we can order that.
It is going to change.
I talked about it with Steve, and Steve, like, rightfully, I think they didn't love any of the options.
I bounced off of him.
I don't think we have a better answer just yet, to be super honest with you.
I saw on that holiday page, they have CV turned up, you'd call it, like, the AI automation platform or something.
I don't know what we want to go with here.
I personally don't love using the word automation.
I think that makes it sound smaller than, like, what many folks are trying to accomplish, because, yeah, so, yeah, I don't know.
I mean, we have a call right after this to, I think, one of them.
of our topics is to go deeper into that.
So we might have an answer for you soon, but we don't already know.
Yeah, I think it's a tough thing.

Like I've worked at so many brands in-house and trying to figure out how to define yourself, especially when you're kind of occupying a new space or a newish space, I think is like one of the toughest things.
How do we talk about this in one sentence that everyone's going to understand?
So anyway, this can be a living thing that we continue to update as you guys kind of hone in on how you want to talk about it.
Yeah, and like in the meantime, I don't know if it's easy for us to go back and update if we ever need to change it.
But for instance, I think if it's going to be a blocker, like description to production API platform, we have in there right now is like it's not inaccurate.
It's fine.
I would love for us to be able to just go and like edit that as a variable across all of our previous posts if we ever need to do that, if it's something we want to use.
Yeah, like I don't think it's a blocker in the sense that it'll stop us from doing things now.
It's only in the sense of wanting to go back and redo them later, depending on how much.
You know, it changes.
So I think that would just be the question.
If it changes significantly, then, you we may want to budget some time to go back and, like, refresh things if we start talking about it in a different way.
But I don't think it has to be a blocker now.
So we did create a new Airtable that has the new topic clusters from the strategy that we talked about.
And we did sort of a first round of keyword research, which I can just show you a little bit of the approach here.
Oops.

So I will clean up this doc before I'm out of office, but I left sort of some of the answers to your questions here because I thought it would really help our team as they think about keyword research and how to, like, go about doing it.
And so our sort of I left at the bottom here kind of the results of our initial competitor gap analysis.
So we went in and looked at sort of that list of competitors.
here.
That you had listed and the keywords they're ranking for that you're not.
And the goal here was kind of to identify some quick wins, like places where I think we think Logic has the potential to start ranking soon.
And then also identify like a comparison content, which is usually like a great place to start is capturing some of those comparison keywords.
So your brand kind of becomes part of that conversation.
And so based on that, we kind of went in and we did our competitor gap research and then thought about kind of where we want to start with our first lane of content.
And so you'll see, oh, I probably have to share a different window.
You'll see in your new Airtable.
Sorry.
No, you're good.
There we go.
go.
Too many windows.
And you'll see that there are about 30 new articles for you to go in and take a look at and approve and leave comments on and just to sort of have us have a place to start.
And ideally, if you could go in this week, I would love to have us run.
And we've just built your new pipeline with the new Artifax, so I would love to have us run a calibration article so we could get it to you this week so you could take a look and see how it's doing.

And my recommendation would be to prioritize one of these comparison articles as our calibration article because I find that sometimes that is where the system can, like, struggle a little bit with, like, properly positioning your brand in comparison.
So I think that's a place where we could catch some of those mistakes.
And then sometimes if we plan to do a lot of these comparison, we
It can be worth just like building an entirely new pipeline set up just for comparisons.
So I think if that's a lane of content we want to kind of lean into a little bit, which I think makes sense here, we might want to actually just build a new pipeline based on your comments on that.
Well, can you clarify what is the exact thing you want me to do?
Yeah.

So basically to go in and review this first round of 30 articles that we recommend on your new Airtable based on your new kind of content strategy pivot and pick one that we should start as considering kind of your calibration article in the same way we did in Strategy Sprint where we tried to do one and have you give heavy feedback on that so we could use that to update our artifacts.
So if you can do that and honestly, if you could just even if now if we align on or soon after this one article to pick, like we can get that going and get it to you by the end of this week.
Honestly, number one looks good.
But just to be clear, you want us to choose one title and basically hit play on it.
going generate an article.
Exactly.
Okay, cool.
Yeah, hit play.
First article to hit play on and then the second thing would be to just review the 30 topics so that way we're set up to start content production again.
I would actually appreciate having a little bit of time to sit with this and review it and get back to you as opposed to doing it on the call.
But like just quick, quick skim, but these look quite reasonable for what I would expect.
this looks, this looks spot on so far.
Then yeah, honestly, and if you just want to pick one and then take your time to review the other ones, that would allow us to get started on that calibration article.
Yeah, my only goal in pushing that one forward is with the holidays coming.
feel like if we could do that one article this week, it would be great.
And we would sort of then be ready and set up after the holidays to like just jump right back into content production.
Heck yeah, no worries at all.
I'm happy to do whatever it takes to get.
Awesome.
Let me see what else was on.
And just to call out, Steve, I'd love for you to as well review the content list and the article I generated.

I think I've been a bit heads down on this, but I think you have, like, a slightly fresher perspective, and I'd just love to get a read on if this is not on your expectations on what kind of stuff we'd be writing about, the right level of technical depth for the right reader.
Like, this past year's model test as well.
Sounds good.
And I can drop this document into Slack after this because it has links to all the things we've been working on in one place to kind of get you up to speed.
Yeah.
And, Steve, I don't, like, think you need to go through and read, like, all the sub documents that, like, up to produce this, like, meta story.
If you want to, great.
I think the right way for Steve to get involved will be to, like, review one of the outputs, and then if there's something wrong there, then we can go back and, like, identify where we got one wrong.
I don't want Fathom take the time to go through each of those documents if it doesn't have to.
Is that cool with you Bailey?
Yeah, that sounds great.
Whatever works for you guys.
Great.
Awesome.
I think that is all of the big things on my list for now.
The only other thing I like to bring up at this point, planning ahead for January, I think there's sort of like two things we would love to do in January.
One is our usual monthly deep dive reporting.

I think given where we are with content production, it probably might make sense to do that towards the end of the month when we've hopefully published a few more and we might have some more signs in terms of early signals for how things are going.
And then the other thing we like to do in early January, if it works with your timeline, is sort of to take some time to have a 2026 planning session.
And then элем
And just like understand any goals or kind of anything that's changed for 2026.
I know you guys just went through this big pivot.
So that timing may or may not make sense for you given what else you have going on.
But it's just a good way for us to keep tabs on kind of what's happening with your company and your goals and figure out how we can support that.
Yeah, I love that.
And then so as far as like timeline for when we start producing articles at the cadence of like five-ish a week that we were in the past, that probably would be in the new years, correct?
Yeah, I think so.
The goal for this week would be to get you that calibration article, have you give feedback on it, and then next week we could update the article and update all our artifacts.
And then, yes, exactly.
So then we would start publishing again at five a week.
Okay, sweet.
And then do you have a rough timeline in mind for when we might get the process of like going back and updating it?
Reviewing previous articles that have been published with like our prior positioning.
I think we can start that now.
I basically just wanted to wait until you had defined that positioning and you were happy with it.
But then we can start anytime.
Sounds good.
Could I review a couple of the ones?
I want to just get a sense of like what's changing and how much is changing before we like go and update all of them en masse.
Yeah.
Can I just give you a couple of those and get a sense of what we're looking at and what we're talking about?
I would find that very just like mentally reassuring.
Do you want me to sort of use this language or do you want me to wait until I kind of hear back from you in terms of how we're defining what logic is before we jump in and start that?
I think it's also okay if we're just going to do like three to start to just to show you what that might look like.
Like, you know, we could use this and like our current artifacts just to give you a sense of what it looks like.
And then, you know, maybe we don't want to update all, you know, 20.
Yeah, I would say let's get started with this language and if we need to revisit that, we can revisit that.
just want to make sure that we're not spending time with outdated language on the internet that may or may not get, you know, picked up and indexed in more and more places.
Yep, for sure.
Okay, so we'll do a few using this and like our current artifacts and send those to you to take a look at and then also send you the calibration article.
And then ideally we can get that all to you this week, for sure the calibration article and then the updates at the very least early next week so you can have it all kind of going into the holidays.
Yeah, sounds good.
Appreciate that.
All right.
Cool, Bailey.
last thing I wanted to loop you in on, just want to make sure you're in, it doesn't change much for y'all, but FYI, it's part of what we do every year, apparently, at Logic.
It's my first year here, so I'm not sure it's every year, but Every year.
Every year.
second winter.
Yeah, all, years.
Because we do like sort of the ideas at the end of the year, things slow down a little bit for most companies.

And instead of trying to, you know, just double down on outbound marketing and stuff, taking a more thoughtful approach or trying to produce like 12 content pieces that are sort of thought leadership, you know, high level things that are meant to grab attention and introduce into conversations where folks are having them on, you know, hacker news and things of that nature.

And so we'll be producing a 12-day, everyday content piece around, you know, again, what's happening in the industry, what's our take on it, what we've learned this year, building an AI infrastructure product, and publishing them on our Substack, which is like where we put our, like, about leadership, engineering, a lot of kind of stuff, yeah.
So shouldn't change much for you, but I to let you know that it's coming.
And if you see a bunch of content come from us, do not be alarmed.
Yeah, that's awesome.
Well, I'll definitely check it out.
I love that.
Thanks.
The 12 days of Christmas.
you guys...
Do publish it on just some Substack or is it on LinkedIn, too?
Do you kind of repurpose it across?
It will be reposted on LinkedIn, yeah.
Yeah, one of the things I'm trying to bring into my own work within all of our work is it's like shamelessly repurpose everywhere, everything.
And so, yeah, it'll be on, it'll be refactored for LinkedIn.
Like, we're actually promoting it on our regular email going out to our user base as well.
So hopefully it'll show up in a couple different places.
Well, that's awesome.
We'll check it out.
And, like, you know, often I know that, you know, thought leadership is a little bit of a different lane than SEO.
But I think being able even to just link out to kind of original material, I think is always, like, great for even for SEO and for LLM as well.

I expect that come early January, once we've, like, done all this and hopefully had at least one or two spike in traffic in some, you know, way, we can then go back and turn those into, like, a, that sounds corny.
mean, don't actually.
We could do this, but like a compiled like ebook that you can download and read all of our thought leadership or something that was like we can use to fuel more SEO powered content as well.
Yeah, for sure.

And it's a it's a it's a question that I'm having kind of across some client accounts, too, which is, you know, what other formats and can we think about about content and how to deliver it and how to help clients get leveraged?
And so it's something I'll keep in mind for for you all, too, as we kind of as a company, start thinking about other other strategic ways we can come in and help.
I think it's a great conversation to have beyond just kind of the SEO blog post format.
Yeah, sounds good.
Awesome.
All right.
Well, have a great holiday.
I'll send you an async update, but I know we won't be seeing each other for a little bit.
So have a great holiday.
Enjoy your boat ride.
Yeah, thank you.
Yeah, that's gonna be exciting.
Yeah, I'm hoping I won't get seasick.
We're gonna see TBD.
See you in January with a fresh new term.
Yeah, there we go.
All right.
See you later.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on holiday ops, new content strategy, and January planning.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=234.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**New Content Pipeline:** A calibration article will be produced this week to test the new pipeline, which uses an API key to automate staging in Logic's CMS for faster, manual review.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=977.0)
  - [**Content Strategy Pivot:** Production will resume at \~5 articles/week in January, focusing on comparison content identified in a competitor gap analysis.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=855.0)
  - [**Repositioning:** "Production API platform" is the working definition for Logic, pending a final decision. This language will be used for new content and to update past articles.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=722.0)
  - [**Image Strategy:** A new two-image system will be adopted: a text-free blog image for better mobile display and a text-overlay social share image for better context.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=640.0)

## Topics

### Content Strategy & Production

  - [**New Topic Clusters:** GrowthX created an Airtable with \~30 new article ideas based on the strategy pivot and a competitor gap analysis.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=855.0)
  - [**Calibration Article:** One comparison article will be produced this week to test the new pipeline and gather feedback on brand positioning.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=977.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Comparison content is a key area where the system can misalign, making it an ideal test case.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=995.0)
      - [**Reviewers:** Azzam and Steve will review for technical depth and strategic alignment.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=1146.0)
  - [**Production Cadence:** Full production (\~5 articles/week) will resume in January.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=1286.0)

### Content Repositioning & Updates

  - [**Logic Definition:** "Production API platform" is the temporary definition.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=722.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Avoids "automation" to prevent understating Logic's value.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=747.0)
      - [**Status:** A final definition is pending an internal Logic meeting.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=722.0)
  - [**Past Article Updates:** GrowthX will update a few past articles with the new language for review.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=1314.0)
      - [**Rationale:** To prevent outdated messaging from being indexed by search engines.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=1381.0)

### Pipeline Automation & Image Strategy

  - [**Automated Staging:** Logic will provide an API key to connect GrowthX's Atlas pipeline to Logic's CMS.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=340.0)
      - [**Benefit:** Eliminates the manual publishing queue, enabling instant staging for review.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=425.0)
  - [**New Image Strategy:** A two-image system will be adopted.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=640.0)
      - [**Blog Image:** A text-free visual for better mobile display.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=640.0)
      - [**Social Share Image:** A text-overlay image for better context on social media.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=640.0)

### January Planning

  - [**Monthly Report:** Scheduled for late January to allow time for new articles to generate performance data.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=1225.0)
  - [**2026 Planning Session:** Proposed for early January to align content strategy with Logic's updated goals.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=1225.0)

### FYI: Logic's "12 Days of Christmas" Campaign

  - [Logic is launching a 12-day thought leadership campaign on Substack and LinkedIn.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=1423.0)
  - [**Goal:** Generate high-level industry conversations.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=1440.0)
  - [**Future Use:** Content may be repurposed into an ebook to fuel more SEO-driven articles.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=1546.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Azzam:**](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=1036.0)
      - [Select one comparison article from the Airtable for the calibration run.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=1036.0)
      - [Review the full list of \~30 new article ideas.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=1093.0)
  - [**Steve:**](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=1146.0)
      - [Provide the API key for automated staging.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=340.0)
      - [Review the calibration article and new topic list.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=1146.0)
  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=1399.0)
      - [Produce the calibration article this week.](https://fathom.video/share/ssSqFyp7Z6_sFAHy-uCarDeRDfzpoCyy?tab=summary&timestamp=977.0)
